title: Object 14
wikitext:
__NOTOC__{{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 14: "Paperstone"}}{{Liminal Theme}}{{Background|file=Paperstone Beach sick.jpg}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Paperstone 2.jpg|caption=Crystal-clear Paperstone found in an unidentified level.}} '''Object 14''', otherwise known as "'''Paperstone'''," is a smooth white sedimentary stone found more noticeably in various aquatic levels across [[the Backrooms]]. It is well-known for its many strange properties. '''Paperstone''' is typically able to resist against extreme conditions like weight, radiation, or pressure due to its high compressive strength and radiation resistance. However, due to its brittleness, it can be effortlessly broken with minimal strength. '''Paperstone''' seems to become stronger and more resistant throughout its formation and in differential erosion. Another well-known property of '''Paperstone''' is its bioluminescence and radioluminescence, emitting a soft blue glow when exposed to high radiation or darkness. The radioluminescence occurs because of phosphorus in the object's composition, which makes it glow under radiation. Meanwhile, the bioluminescence is caused by fungi and microorganisms that grow on '''Paperstone''' during its formation and after. This especially happens due to the wet environment that surrounds most instances of the object. '''Paperstone''' is often considered a mixture of various rocks, such as sandstones like itacolumite, salt rocks, calcite, bioluminescent rocks, diorite, and others. However, the full composition of '''Paperstone''' remains a mystery. For various reasons, '''Paperstone''''s composition and properties can be effortlessly changed, resulting in objects like [[Level 865|Papersap]], a molten and liquid variation found in [[Level 865]]. Another example is Paperstone Glass, a thin and crystallized instance found underwater or in tundras. Paperstone Glass is a sought-after material in aquatic levels as an easier-to-find replacement for regular glass. It is unknown how Paperstone Grass is exactly formed. It is known that '''Paperstone''' undergoes a process of crystallization under exceedingly cold temperatures; this process is important to its formation, seeing as Paperstone Glass is crystallized. However, tests did not result in a substance like Paperstone Glass. The name '''Paperstone''' comes from the rock's paper-white coloration, its malleability, and how effortlessly it breaks with human strength. ==Usage== {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Paperstones_.jpg|caption=Small Paperstones.}} '''Paperstone''' is generally used as a basic building material for its frequency and resistance. It is used in many communities across [[the Backrooms]] as a low-cost, easy-to-find replacement for bricks, cement, glass, and other materials. It is especially used in safer and more peaceful communities, where the lack of delinquents and criminals makes upgrading to a better material unnecessary. '''Paperstone''' is also commonly used for arts and crafts as accessories, decorations, and souvenirs, such as necklaces and rings. It is popular in that field due to its bioluminescence, which makes for glowing and fashionable accessories for consumers to use. '''Paperstone''' products tend to be very cheap, leading them to be among the most purchased items in the market. It is also used for fighting and building—and most commonly—research tools like flashlights, radiation detectors, and knives, seeing as it has qualities similar to the materials more typically used to make these. This object tends to be a go-to option for flashlights, as it works efficiently in dark levels—glowing more in even darker areas—and is natural, meaning it does not have to be charged. These flashlights can also be used to detect radiation, seeing as this object also glows. '''Paperstone''''s resistance makes for high-quality hammers and knives that last as long as iron and other metals. ==Locations== It is very common to encounter '''Paperstone''' across [[the Backrooms]]; it is one of the most widespread objects in the dimension. '''Paperstone''' is most typically found forming along the coastline, next to large bodies of water like oceans, seas, or large lakes (most commonly in [[Level 100]], [[Level 352]], [[Level 7]], [[Level 67]], or [[Blue Horizon|the Blue Horizon]]). However, it can still form in shallow bodies of water, only more rarely. It is also rarely found in shallow caves in the form of stalagmites and other formations. It is also found in the form of a level's architecture (e.g., [[Level 385]] and [[Level 4]]) or as smaller pieces trashed or hidden throughout the place. ==Formation== {{Blurimage|align=right|caption=A beach with many Paperstones.|file=Paperstone Beach sick.jpg}} '''Paperstone''' seems to form as a result of erosion and accumulation of particles from various types of stone over the years, like salt rocks and sandstones, among other substances that constitute its composition. Over time, these particles precipitate and are cemented together, thus becoming '''Paperstone'''. All of these substances grant '''Paperstone''' its unique properties. Some pieces of these formations break apart due to erosion from the coast's waves and are smoothened and broken apart by the running water, eventually resurfacing as the pebbles usually found by humans.
